steps.title: Calculate Andrew's mean
steps.content:
Sum Andrew's six measurements: $2.4 + 2.5 + 3.6 + 3.1 + 2.5 + 2.7 = 16.8$ kilograms.
Mean: $\frac{16.8}{6} = 2.8$ kilograms

steps.title: Set up Maria's mean equation
steps.content:
Sum Maria's five known values: $3.1 + 2.7 + 2.9 + 3.3 + 2.8 = 14.8$ kilograms.
Maria's total with $x$: $x + 14.8$
Maria's mean: $\frac{x + 14.8}{6}$
Given: Maria's mean is 0.1 kg greater than Andrew's mean, so it equals $2.8 + 0.1 = 2.9$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Set the equation: $$\frac{x + 14.8}{6} = 2.9$$
Multiply both sides by 6: $$x + 14.8 = 17.4$$
Subtract 14.8: $$x = 2.6$$
That is the value of x — 2.6
